techniques.evidence_quote: an Amazon AWS S3 bucket belonging to the LA Times had been left wide open, granting global write-access to anyone who fancied dropping their code on the server
an unauthorised third party had taken advantage of the sloppy cloud security, and meddled with a Javascript file
the miner was throttled to reduce the impact on visitors' CPUs and make it harder for users to spot that something was awry
Coinhive cryptomining code was added to an LA Times website called 'The Homicide Report'

attack_chain.description: Attacker discovered a publicly misconfigured S3 bucket belonging to the LA Times with global write-access enabled, allowing unauthenticated modification of hosted files.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: an Amazon AWS S3 bucket belonging to the LA Times had been left wide open, granting global write-access to anyone who fancied dropping their code on the server

attack_chain.description: Attacker wrote malicious Coinhive cryptomining JavaScript into a JS file hosted in the S3 bucket, which was then served to all visitors of the LA Times Homicide Report website.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: an unauthorised third party had taken advantage of the sloppy cloud security, and meddled with a Javascript file. In this way, Coinhive cryptomining code was added to an LA Times website

attack_chain.description: The injected Coinhive script executed in visitors' browsers, mining Monero cryptocurrency using their CPU resources. The miner was intentionally throttled to avoid detection.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the miner was throttled to reduce the impact on visitors' CPUs and make it harder for users to spot that something was awry. Perhaps this is how the attack appears to have gone unnoticed since February 9th

initial_access_vector: Publicly misconfigured S3 bucket with global write-access enabled, allowing any unauthenticated party to upload or modify hosted files
persistence_mechanism: Malicious Coinhive JavaScript injected into an existing JS file hosted on the S3 bucket, served continuously to all website visitors
